name: Gerês-Xurés and Lindoso Reservoir Trail
name_original: Bande – vuelta por Lobios – río Lima y Aquis Querquennis – Reserva de Biosfera Gerês-Xurés
description: This difficult 47.5-mile touring bicycle route crosses the Gerês-Xurés Biosphere Reserve, featuring Roman ruins and mountain views.
region_intro: Touring cycling routes around Bande traverse a landscape characterized by varied terrain, including areas near reservoirs and historical sites. The region offers routes that range from moderate loops around ancient dolmens to more challenging trails through natural parks. Cyclists can expect a mix of rolling hills and more significant ascents, providing diverse experiences. Best touring cycling routes around Bande The most popular touring cycling route is Gerês-Xurés and Lindoso Reservoir Trail, a 47.4 miles (76.4 km) trail that takes 5 hours 18 minutes to complete. This difficult route features significant elevation gain through a natural park and near a reservoir. Another top favourite among local touring cyclists is Dolmen of Casola do Foxo – Dolmen Casota do Foxo loop from Igrexa de Santa Comba de Bande, a difficult 28.8 miles (46.4 km) path. This route includes substantial climbing and passes by historical dolmens. Local touring cyclists also love the Bike loop from Torre da Forxa, a 27.7 miles (44.6 km) trail leading through varied terrain, often completed in about 2 hours 37 minutes. Touring cycling around Bande is defined by routes exploring historical dolmens, reservoir areas, and natural park landscapes. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate loops to more challenging long-distance trails. The routes in Bande are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 5.0 stars from over 1 review. More than 30 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore Bande's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on touring cycling routes around Bande?
A: Touring cycling routes around Bande feature varied terrain, including areas near reservoirs and historical sites. You can expect a mix of rolling hills and more significant ascents, especially on routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1017191596/geres-xures-and-lindoso-reservoir-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Gerês-Xurés and Lindoso Reservoir Trail&lt;/a&gt;, which has significant elevation gain through a natural park.

Q: Are there any easier touring cycling routes in Bande for beginners or those seeking a more relaxed ride?
A: Yes, while some routes are challenging, there are moderate options available. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20147806" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Dolmen of Casola do Foxo – Dolmen Casota do Foxo loop from Os Outeiros&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 8.5-mile (13.7 km) path with less elevation gain, suitable for a more relaxed experience.

Q: What historical sites or natural landmarks can I see while cycling in Bande?
A: Bande's touring cycling routes often pass by significant historical and natural attractions. You can explore ancient dolmens, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2479245" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Dolmen of Casola do Foxo&lt;/a&gt;, and visit historical sites like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3112491" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Aquis Querquennis Roman Camp&lt;/a&gt;. Natural features include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3557474" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Bico do Patelo&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2385913" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pozo Caído Waterfall&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any circular touring cycling routes available in Bande?
A: Yes, many of the touring cycling routes in Bande are designed as loops. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31442019" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Bike loop from Torre da Forxa&lt;/a&gt; is a 27.7-mile (44.6 km) circular trail, and there are several loops around the Dolmen of Casola do Foxo, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20147829" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Dolmen Casota do Foxo – Dolmen of Casola do Foxo loop from As Maus&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What is the best time of year to go touring cycling in Bande?
A: While specific seasonal data for Bande isn't provided, regions with similar landscapes often offer pleasant cycling conditions in spring and autumn. These seasons typically provide milder temperatures and less extreme weather, making them ideal for exploring the varied terrain and historical sites.

Q: Are there any family-friendly touring cycling routes in Bande?
A: For families, routes with moderate difficulty and shorter distances are recommended. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20147806" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Dolmen of Casola do Foxo – Dolmen Casota do Foxo loop from Os Outeiros&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate option that could be suitable for families looking for a less strenuous ride, allowing them to enjoy the scenery and historical dolmens.

Q: Can I bring my dog on touring cycling routes in Bande?
A: While komoot routes don't specify dog-friendliness, many natural parks and outdoor areas allow dogs on trails, often requiring them to be on a leash. It's always best to check local regulations for specific natural parks or protected areas you plan to cycle through, such as the Gerês-Xurés Natural Park, to ensure compliance.

Q: What do other touring cyclists enjoy the most about cycling in Bande?
A: The touring cycling routes in Bande are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 5.0 stars from over 1 review. Cyclists appreciate the diverse terrain, the exploration of historical dolmens, and the scenic reservoir areas and natural park landscapes.

Q: Are there opportunities for longer, more challenging touring cycling adventures in Bande?
A: Absolutely. For experienced touring cyclists seeking a challenge, routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1017191596/geres-xures-and-lindoso-reservoir-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Gerês-Xurés and Lindoso Reservoir Trail&lt;/a&gt; offer a difficult 47.4-mile (76.4 km) journey with significant elevation gain through a natural park and near a reservoir. Another challenging option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/32142972" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Dolmen of Casola do Foxo – Dolmen Casota do Foxo loop from Igrexa de Santa Comba de Bande&lt;/a&gt;, a 28.8-mile (46.4 km) path with substantial climbing.

Q: How many touring cycling routes are available around Bande?
A: There are 8 touring cycling routes available around Bande, offering a range of experiences from moderate loops to more difficult long-distance trails. These routes explore historical dolmens, reservoir areas, and natural park landscapes.

Q: Are there any bridges or unique architectural features to see on the routes?
A: Yes, the region around Bande features interesting bridges. You might encounter the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4042827" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Old Bridge of Castro Laboreiro&lt;/a&gt; or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4519446" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Varziela Bridge&lt;/a&gt;, which is near the charming Varziela Village, adding historical and scenic elements to your ride.
tips: Ascent of 3.3 km from Bande to Alto de Lermes gaining 166 m of unevenness with a comfortable average slope of 4.3%. Don't forget to check out the views of the surrounding landscape.

Lobios is a small town dating from Roman times, on the old Portuguese trade route that crosses the Sierra de Xures following the Lima River and the hot springs that surround the area. Good place to stop for a coffee.
Crossing the Lima Lindoso river reservoir, you can see the submerged town of Aceredo when the waters are low.

Spectacular Baroque architectural work completed in the mid-18th century located in the town of A Terrachá. The temple stands out for its monumental stone façade.
The complex is made up of three naves and a transept in the central nave, covered with a dome.

Take a detour from the main road to see this historical wonder Aquis Querquennis is a ruin of a Roman city. The remaining walls and arched gates on the banks of the Limia River reservoir provide a vivid reminder of the life that once was.

faqs.question: How challenging is the Bande – return trip via Lobios – river Lima and Aquis Querquennis – Gerês-Xurés Biosphere Reserve trail for touring cyclists?
faqs.answer: This route is classified as difficult and requires a good to very good level of fitness for touring cyclists. You can expect mountainous terrain with significant elevation gains, including sections with over 1,400 meters of ascent. While many parts are on quiet, well-paved mountain roads, there are also segments with gravel tracks, particularly along ancient Roman roads. Some unpaved sections might even require you to push your bike. E-bikes can make the climbs and longer distances more accessible.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain can I expect on this touring bicycle route?
faqs.answer: The terrain is varied, offering a mix of surfaces. You will cycle on quiet mountain roads, which are mostly well-paved. However, be prepared for gravel tracks, especially when following sections of the ancient Via Nova. There may also be unpaved and difficult segments where pushing your bike could be necessary. The route traverses hilly relief with plateaus, glacier valleys, and steep slopes around the Lima River.

faqs.question: What are the main points of interest along the route?
faqs.answer: This trail offers a wealth of natural beauty and historical sites. Key highlights include the Alto de Lermes, the charming Lobios Village (known for its natural thermal springs), and the Lindoso Bridge and View of Submerged Aceredo Village. You'll also pass the Church of Santa María a Real de A Terrachá and the significant archaeological site of Aquis Querquennis Roman Camp, which is particularly striking when reservoir levels are low.

faqs.question: Where can I park and access the trailhead for this touring bicycle route?
faqs.answer: The tour starts and ends in Bande. You can typically find parking within the village of Bande to begin your ride. For specific parking locations near Aquis Querquennis, there are usually designated areas for visitors to the Roman camp, which can serve as an alternative starting point if you wish to focus on that section.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to cycle this route?
faqs.answer: The Gerês-Xurés Biosphere Reserve region is best enjoyed during spring and autumn. These seasons offer pleasant temperatures for cycling and showcase the diverse flora and fauna. Summer can be quite hot, especially in the valleys, while winter may bring colder temperatures and potentially challenging conditions in the mountainous areas.

faqs.question: Are there any permits or fees required to cycle within the Gerês-Xurés Biosphere Reserve?
faqs.answer: Generally, there are no specific permits or entrance fees required for cycling on public trails within the Gerês-Xurés Transboundary Biosphere Reserve, which encompasses both Portugal's Peneda-Gerês National Park and Spain's Baixa Limia-Serra do Xurés Natural Park. However, it's always advisable to respect local regulations, stay on marked paths, and be mindful of protected areas. Some specific activities or access to certain private lands might require permission, but for general touring cycling, it's usually unrestricted.

faqs.question: Is the trail dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: While dogs are generally allowed in many parts of the Gerês-Xurés Biosphere Reserve, specific rules may apply within the Peneda-Gerês National Park (Portugal) and Baixa Limia-Serra do Xurés Natural Park (Spain). It is crucial to keep dogs on a leash at all times to protect wildlife and livestock, and to clean up after them. Always check local signage for any specific restrictions in certain areas or during particular seasons.

faqs.question: Does this touring bicycle route intersect with other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, this route incorporates sections of several other paths. You will find yourself on parts of the Caminho da Geira e dos Arrieiros, Camiño Natural de San Rosendo, Camiño do Mangueiro, Camiño de Gusteu, Camiño do Ribeiro, Camiño da Piteira, Camiño da Portela, Estrada da Mariposa, Pista do Refuxio, Camiño da Quebrada, Camiño de Torno, Camiño da Barrela, and Camiño da Capela.

faqs.question: What makes this cross-border cycling experience unique?
faqs.answer: The unique aspect of this route is its seamless traverse between Portugal's Peneda-Gerês National Park and Spain's Baixa Limia-Serra do Xurés Natural Park, both part of the UNESCO Gerês-Xurés Cross-Border Biosphere Reserve. This allows you to experience the distinct culture, nature, and cuisine of both countries within a single trip, often following sections of the ancient Roman Via Nova (Road XVIII) with its historical milestones.

faqs.question: Are there opportunities for food and drink along the way?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route passes through villages like Lobios Village, where you can find amenities such as cafes and restaurants. Given the rural nature of some sections, it's advisable to carry sufficient water and snacks, especially between villages, but opportunities for refreshments are available in the larger settlements.

faqs.question: What should I bring for a touring bicycle trip on this trail?
faqs.answer: Given the challenging nature and varied terrain, it's recommended to bring a well-maintained touring bicycle, appropriate cycling gear, and a helmet. Essential items include sufficient water, snacks, sun protection, a basic repair kit, and navigation tools. Due to the cross-border nature, remember to carry identification. Layers of clothing are advisable due to potential weather changes in mountainous areas.
